title: AST Assault: Fairbanks [AK25049859]
summary: On May 24, 2025, at approximately 1915 hours, Fairbanks AST assisted Fairbanks Police Department in a foot pursuit in the area of 27th Avenue and Wilson Street, resulting in the arrest of Jeremiah Bell, age 20, for assault.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

title: AST Assault IV DV / VCOR / Vio Custodian Duties: Mentasta Lake [AK25048870]
incident_type: Assault IV DV / VCOR / Vio Custodian Duties
summary: On 05/22/2025 at approximately 0712 hours, Alaska State Troopers received a request for medical assistance in the Mentasta village. AST further learned there was an allegation of a physical fight between siblings. Investigation revealed Derrick J. Neal, age 39, assaulted his brother. Further investigation revealed a Violation of Conditions of Release on Neal’s brother as well as Violation of Custodian Duties on Neal’s brother’s third-party custodian. Neal was remanded to Fairbanks Correctional Center and held without bail. The other two charges were submitted to the District Attorney’s Office.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch
topic: Assault IV DV / VCOR / Vio Custodian Duties

entities.address_detail: of Custodian Duties on Neal’s brother’s third-p

title: AST Vehicle Theft: Wasilla [AK25049795]
summary: On 05/24/2025 at approximately 1640 hours, Alaska State Troopers contacted David Waite, 45, of Wasilla, near Big Lake. Following the investigation, Waite was arrested for vehicle theft in the first degree and remanded to Mat-Su Pretrial without incident.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

charges: vehicle theft in the first degree

title: AST Assault I, MIW IV: Wasilla [AK25049645]
summary: On 5.24.25 at approximately 0700 hours, Alaska State Troopers in Palmer responded to a residence on Bull Moose Drive for a report of a male who had been shot. title: Arctic Sea Ice Extent: May 22, 2025
summary: May 22, 2025: The Arctic sea ice has experienced a smaller decrease for this week of 2.41% (last week’s decrease rate was 3.76%), from the level on 05/15/2025 of 12.548 M km 2 to 12.246 M km 2 for this week. The sea ice extent for 2025 to date is now in second place being inched out by the sea ice e...

title: AST MICS: Fairbanks [AK25049618]
summary: On 05/24/2025 at 0131 hours Troopers responded to a call regarding individuals doing narcotics in the bathroom at the Circle K gas station on Peger Road. A Trooper contacted an adult female, and ultimately methamphetamine was seized from her. Charges will be pending results from the state crime lab.
sourceAttribution: Source: Alaska State Troopers Daily Dispatch

entities.address_detail: the Circle K gas station on Peger Road

title: TCC Responds to Discriminatory Remarks Made by Fairbanks Mayor David Pruhs
summary: In April, Tanana Chiefs Conference (TCC) responded to a Facebook post by Fairbanks Mayor David Pruhs that deeply disparaged many in our communities — especially rural Alaskans and Alaska Native people. The post, made after a walk-through of Golden Heart Plaza, referenced people “coming in from the v...

title: TCC’s Public Safety Manager Appointed to Dunleavy Administration’s MMIP Review Commission
summary: David Atteberry, Tanana Chiefs Conference’s (TCC) Public Safety Manager, has been appointed by Governor Dunleavy to the administration’s Missing and Murdered Indigenous Persons (MMIP) Review Commission. Formed earlier this year, the nine-member commission reviews unsolved MMIP cases and reports find...

title: TCC Leadership Visits Anvik and Nikolai
summary: On April 30, 2025, Tanana Chiefs Conference (TCC) Leadership and departmental staff traveled to the villages of Anvik and Nikolai to meet with Tribal leaders and community members.
